understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to use a limited number of steps to move the selectable piece(s) on the grid to reach the goal tile(s) (marked as +), likely requiring positioning or moving obstacles to open a path.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, but moves could fail when blocked by walls or objects. Some adjacent objects could be pushable, moving along with the selected piece when pushed in the movement direction. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK on a selectable’s exact pixel position switched control to that piece. The movement_blocked hints only indicated nearby adjacency and didn’t guarantee that a move would or wouldn’t succeed, so actual interaction was needed to learn wall/obstacle behavior.
